rows.notes: Included from gemini-flash. This faint year header is present in the top right of the page and was missed by gpt-5-mini. Confirmed visually.
Selected gemini-flash's description 'Recepta' as it is a more accurate reading of the Latin compared to gpt-5-mini's 'Receipts'. Both correctly identified as a header with no amounts.

Selected gemini-flash for its accurate reading of the pounds amount '1480', which is visually distinct from gpt-5-mini's '4680'. The first digit is clearly a '1' in the image. Description text refined to 'Brot. over' based on image. Row type 'entry' is suitable for a brought over balance, which is not a final total.
rows.english_description: Brought forward balance of receipts from the previous page.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Brought forward balance of receipts, amounting to £1480 12s 4d.

rows.notes: Extracted as a separate header based on gpt-5-mini's interpretation and visual confirmation. The date 'Oct.12.' is distinct on the left margin, indicating a new section or date group, and not part of the description of the following entry as gemini-flash had merged it.

rows.description: Of Mr Barnes on Acct. of Wild Parish. viz
rows.notes: Selected gpt-5-mini's description for more accurate reading of 'Parish' over gemini-flash's 'avish'. Both correctly identify no monetary amounts for this descriptive line.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Mr Barnes on account of Wild parish (amount not stated).

rows.description: Rent due at Xmas. 1824. 3 yrs &c. at 24 £ pr an.
rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash as it correctly identifies that this descriptive line has no associated monetary amount in the ledger columns. gpt-5-mini incorrectly associated an amount ('81.3.0') and misread parts of the description ('1827', '24/6'). Visual verification confirms '1824' and '24 £'.
rows.english_description: Rent due at Christmas 1824 covering three years at £24 per year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent due at Christmas 1824, three years at £24 per year (total £72).
